top10.introHtml: Refresh run 2026-07-16. All three sources scraped. Cars.com returned 18 clean-title/no-accident listings (page 2 Cloudflare-blocked — degraded). CarGurus returned 22 listings (pages 1+2). AutoTrader returned 16 listings (page 1 of ~50 total; no Cloudflare block). Excluded as 2-seaters (not in original deny list but confirmed by model knowledge): Toyota Paseo 1997 and Ford Thunderbird 2002/2004 (2002–2005 generation was a 2-seat roadster). TrueCar: statically skipped per task rules.

picks.whyGood: The B8 A5 Cabriolet hits the sweet spot for this price range: proper rear seats, a well-damped soft top, Audi MMI, and a 2.0T TFSI that responds well to basic maintenance. At 57K miles this example sits comfortably in early-life territory for the platform. Cars.com clean title and no-accidents confirmation make it the standout low-risk pick.
picks.whatToVerify: Full top cycle 3× (up and down); inspect headliner corners for water staining; check rear window zipper seam; scan for MMI faults (B8 infotainment is known to fail); verify cam-chain tensioner TSB was applied; inspect rocker panels and rear wheel arches for early rust — lift the car.
picks.bottomLine: Best all-around value in the pool: low miles, clean history, proven platform.

picks.whyGood: The F57 MINI Convertible (third-gen, launched 2009 as R57, refreshed 2015 as F57 equivalent) brings a modern interior, much-improved build quality over earlier Minis, and a solid B38/B46 turbocharged engine. At 55K miles and under $10K with a Cars.com clean title, it offers a fun, premium-feeling drop-top at a budget price.
picks.whatToVerify: Confirm model year is R57 (2009-2015) or true F57 (2016+) — impacts which engine family. Check soft-top motor for slow operation; inspect boot seal; verify no water in spare tire well; check for oil leaks around valve cover (R57 N16/N18 common). Ask for full service history — timing chain updates are critical.
picks.bottomLine: Fun, modern, and value-priced — the pick for someone who wants a daily-driver drop-top under $10K.

picks.whyGood: A 22-year-old car with 45,587 miles is a legitimate rarity. The B6 A4 Cabriolet is a handsome, properly structured soft-top with Audi refinement. At this mileage the drivetrain is essentially broken-in, not worn out. Clean title confirmed on Cars.com. The caveat is age: seals and rubber components are old regardless of mileage.
picks.whatToVerify: Age-related seals: convertible top weatherstripping, window seals, coolant hoses, timing belt (due by age not mileage — likely past due on a 2004), serpentine belt, thermostat. Check the hydraulic top mechanism for slow operation or leaks. Inspect the soft-top fabric for dry rot or cracking. Compression test recommended given age.
picks.bottomLine: Ultra-low-mileage time capsule — genuinely rare at this price, but budget for a full rubber-and-fluids refresh.

picks.whyGood: The E93 328i Convertible is the most reliable and driver-focused 4-seat soft-top BMW has offered at this price. The N52/N51 inline-six is bulletproof when maintained, the M3-derived hydraulic top cycles in 22 seconds, and the chassis balance is outstanding. At 70K miles and under $10K, this is a driver's car at a mechanic's price.
picks.whatToVerify: Cooling system: expansion tank, water pump, thermostat housing (all plastic — replace proactively if not done). Check top motor for slow cycling. Inspect rear window defroster traces. Verify valve cover gaskets and oil separator/CCV not leaking. Scan for ABS and DSC faults. Ask for brake fluid and coolant dates.
picks.bottomLine: The driver's pick: silk-smooth six-cylinder, near-perfect chassis, and still under $10K.

picks.whyGood: The B7 A4 Cabriolet at 62K miles is a low-stress find at $10,990. Both CarGurus and AutoTrader list this car, and Cars.com confirmed clean title and no accidents. The 2.0T FSI on the B7 platform is torquier and more refined than the B6, and 62K miles puts this well inside the reliability window for this engine.
picks.whatToVerify: Carbon buildup on intake valves is the #1 FSI issue on this engine — ask about walnut blasting history. Check the DL501 DSG or 6-speed for smooth operation. Inspect soft-top hydraulic lines under the rear seat — common slow leak location. Check multifunction steering wheel and MMI. Verify front strut mounts (common B7 rattle source).
picks.bottomLine: Low miles, multiple-source confirmed — solid premium pick with one engine quirk to ask about.

picks.whyGood: The only confirmed manual transmission in the entire pool makes this 2007 MINI Cooper the definitive driver's choice. At 65,594 miles the R57 is well within its useful life. The 6-speed manual is gearbox-swap simple to maintain, and the go-kart handling is unmatched among 4-seat convertibles at this price. At $8,452 it's also the second-cheapest pick in the top 10.
picks.whatToVerify: Top seal at A-pillars (water ingress common on R57); check headliner and carpet behind rear seats for moisture; verify electric top fully latches at both front latches; inspect clutch for slip; check timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start (critical N16/N18 item); look for power steering rack leaks.
picks.bottomLine: The enthusiast pick: the only stick-shift in the pool, sub-$9K, 65K miles — buy it and enjoy it.

picks.whyGood: A B7 S4 Cabriolet with a 4.2L V8 at $7,204 is the most bang-for-the-dollar listing in this entire search — $7K for a genuine V8 German luxury convertible. Cars.com confirms clean title and no accidents. The caveat: the B7 S4 V8 is notoriously expensive to maintain (timing chains, valve lifters, B-service intervals). Buy it with eyes open and a budget for deferred work.
picks.whatToVerify: Timing chain service status (critical on 4.2L — listen for rattle on cold start; repair is $3-4K labor-intensive). Check for valve lifter tick. Verify rear differential fluid and quattro rear diff. Inspect top for proper rear window alignment. Pull codes — the 4.2L generates EVAP and cam-timing codes when maintenance is overdue.
picks.bottomLine: Best price-per-thrill in the pool — a true V8 convertible at hatchback money, but have a mechanic pre-inspect first.

picks.whyGood: The 8V A3 Cabriolet is the most modern Audi in this search — with virtual cockpit, updated MMI, and a refined 1.8T TFSI (or 2.0T on higher trims). At $11,999 and 83K miles, it offers the newest technology and most current safety features in the pool. Cars.com clean title and no accidents confirmed.
picks.whatToVerify: Check soft-top tension cables — known to snap on 8V Cabriolets, causing incomplete closure. Verify MMI/virtual cockpit boots without errors. Inspect for oil consumption (some EA888 engines in this era had documented consumption). Check DSG service interval. Confirm top drain tube routing — blockages cause rear footwell flooding.
picks.bottomLine: Most modern Audi in the pool — newest tech, clean history, reasonable miles.

picks.dealer: Jeff D'Ambrosio Chrysler Dodge Jeep Mits
picks.whyGood: The Eos is uniquely positioned: a power-retractable hardtop in a 4-seat package, offering coupe solidity with convertible sun. The 2012 runs the EA113 2.0T FSI — the same proven base as the GTI and A4. At 76K miles and $12,385 with Cars.com clean title and no accidents, it's the most practical pick in the top 10 for year-round use.
picks.whatToVerify: Roof seals at the center join point — Eos tops are complex (5-piece folding hardtop) and leaks concentrate there. Test all 5 roof segments cycle completely. Inspect the rear glass latch. Check for sunroof drain blockage (Eos has 2 drain paths that back up). Verify DSG service at 40K intervals. Carbon buildup on FSI intake (same as A4 above).
picks.bottomLine: The all-weather pick: a hardtop convertible at a soft-top price, clean history, sensible miles.

picks.whyGood: The B8 S5 Cabriolet with the supercharged 3.0T V6 is a genuine grand tourer at an entry-level price. At $10,900 and 91,900 miles, this is the highest-performance car in the Top 10. Cars.com confirms clean title and no accidents. The 3.0 TFSI supercharger is notably more reliable than the twin-turbo found in later S5 models.
picks.whatToVerify: Supercharger whine or hesitation on acceleration (failing coupler — listen carefully). Check cam-chain tensioners (same concern as A4/A5 on this era). Inspect top for hydraulic line condition. Scan for all stored codes — S5s with deferred maintenance accumulate them. Brake rotors and pads wear faster due to weight; check condition.
picks.bottomLine: The performance pick: supercharged V6 grand tourer under $11K — exceptional value if the top has been maintained.

manual10.introHtml: Only one confirmed manual-transmission listing found across all three sources: the 2007 MINI Cooper from Josie's Auto Sales (confirmed Manual on AutoTrader). Many Cars.com listings had no transmission data captured. If you want a stick-shift, this is the pick — and it's excellent.

picks.whyGood: The only confirmed manual transmission in the entire pool makes this 2007 MINI Cooper the definitive driver's choice. At 65,594 miles the R57 is well within its useful life. The 6-speed manual is gearbox-swap simple to maintain, and the go-kart handling is unmatched among 4-seat convertibles at this price. At $8,452 it's also the second-cheapest pick in the top 10.
picks.whatToVerify: Top seal at A-pillars (water ingress common on R57); check headliner and carpet behind rear seats for moisture; verify electric top fully latches at both front latches; inspect clutch for slip; check timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start (critical N16/N18 item); look for power steering rack leaks.
picks.bottomLine: The enthusiast pick: the only stick-shift in the pool, sub-$9K, 65K miles — buy it and enjoy it.

mustangs.introHtml: One 2005+ Mustang found in this search window ($7K–$15K, 35mi of 19063): the 2012 V6 Convertible from CarGurus. Pre-2005 examples (1987 Fox body, 1994 SN-95) appeared in the pool but are excluded per the 2005+ (S197+) rule.

picks.whyGood: The S197 V6 Mustang Convertible (2011-2014 refresh) is a significant upgrade over earlier V6s — the 3.7L Cyclone makes 305hp and is genuinely fun. At $10,995 and 96K miles, this is a fair entry for a pony car convertible with modern equipment. The S197 chassis is proven and well-documented for DIY maintenance.
picks.whatToVerify: Check convertible top motor and seals at A-pillars (common S197 leak point). Inspect for subframe rust (S197s rust early at the rear subframe mounts in salt states). Verify 3.7L for no oil consumption issues. Check for rear differential whine. Manual top models: inspect bow pins and weatherstripping tension.
picks.bottomLine: The only 2005+ Mustang convertible in this search — solid V6 pony car at a fair price.
